channel.title: CWE-601 on ZAP
channel.description: Recent content in CWE-601 on ZAP

item.title: Anchor href manipulated from tainted source
item.description:
Tainted value assigned to href attribute.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Client-side redirect via history.pushState
item.description:
Tainted URL passed to history.pushState, altering client-side navigation.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Client-side redirect via location.assign
item.description:
Tainted destination URL passed to location.assign.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Client-side redirect via location.href
item.description:
Tainted data assigned to location.href, causing a client-side redirect.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Client-side redirect via location.replace
item.description:
Tainted destination URL passed to location.replace.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Client-side route change via history.replaceState
item.description:
Tainted URL passed to history.replaceState, altering client-side navigation state.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Disallow direct navigation primitives
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: DOM-based Open Redirection (taint flow)
item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module

item.title: External Redirect
item.description: URL redirectors represent common functionality employed by web sites to forward an incoming request to an alternate resource. This can be done for a variety of reasons and is often done to allow resources to be moved within the directory structure and to avoid breaking functionality for users that request the resource at its previous location. URL redirectors may also be used to implement load balancing, leveraging abbreviated URLs or recording outgoing links. It is this last implementation which is often used in phishing attacks as described in the example below. URL redirectors do not necessarily represent a direct security vulnerability but can be abused by attackers trying to social engineer victims into believing that they are navigating to a site other than the true destination.

item.title: External Redirect
item.description: URL redirectors represent common functionality employed by web sites to forward an incoming request to an alternate resource. This can be done for a variety of reasons and is often done to allow resources to be moved within the directory structure and to avoid breaking functionality for users that request the resource at its previous location. URL redirectors may also be used to implement load balancing, leveraging abbreviated URLs or recording outgoing links. It is this last implementation which is often used in phishing attacks as described in the example below. URL redirectors do not necessarily represent a direct security vulnerability but can be abused by attackers trying to social engineer victims into believing that they are navigating to a site other than the true destination.

item.title: External Redirect
item.description: URL redirectors represent common functionality employed by web sites to forward an incoming request to an alternate resource. This can be done for a variety of reasons and is often done to allow resources to be moved within the directory structure and to avoid breaking functionality for users that request the resource at its previous location. URL redirectors may also be used to implement load balancing, leveraging abbreviated URLs or recording outgoing links. It is this last implementation which is often used in phishing attacks as described in the example below. URL redirectors do not necessarily represent a direct security vulnerability but can be abused by attackers trying to social engineer victims into believing that they are navigating to a site other than the true destination.

item.title: External Redirect
item.description: URL redirectors represent common functionality employed by web sites to forward an incoming request to an alternate resource. This can be done for a variety of reasons and is often done to allow resources to be moved within the directory structure and to avoid breaking functionality for users that request the resource at its previous location. URL redirectors may also be used to implement load balancing, leveraging abbreviated URLs or recording outgoing links. It is this last implementation which is often used in phishing attacks as described in the example below. URL redirectors do not necessarily represent a direct security vulnerability but can be abused by attackers trying to social engineer victims into believing that they are navigating to a site other than the true destination.

item.title: Form action manipulated by tainted route or body input
item.description:
Tainted route, body, or messaging value changed form action.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: navigation.navigate redirect from tainted source
item.description:
Tainted value passed to navigation.navigate.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Off-site Redirect
item.description: Open redirects are one of the OWASP 2010 Top Ten vulnerabilities. This check looks at user-supplied input in query string parameters and POST data to identify where open redirects might be possible. Open redirects occur when an application allows user-supplied input (e.g. https://nottrusted.com) to control an off-site destination. This is generally a pretty accurate way to find where 301 or 302 redirects could be exploited by spammers or phishing attacks.

item.title: Open redirect reflected in body destination
item.description:
Tests for open redirect by forcing redirect-like parameters to an external, benign domain.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Open redirect reflected in form action
item.description:
Tests for open redirect by forcing redirect-like parameters to an external, benign domain.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Open redirect via common param names
item.description:
Tests for open redirect by forcing redirect-like parameters to an external, benign domain.

Generated by OWASP PTK DAST Module

item.title: Open redirect via Navigation API
item.description:
Tainted destination URL used in navigation.navigate.

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.title: Open redirect via window.open
item.description:
Tainted URL used in window.open (possible open redirect).

Generated by OWASP PTK IAST Module

item.description:
Detects client-side code that can redirect users to attacker-controlled URLs (open redirects). Includes assignment/calls that control window/location/navigation, attr-based redirects, form actions and jQuery variants.

Generated by OWASP PTK SAST Module
